column1: 121|I need to check my bag before lunch.|점심 전에 가방을 확인해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when you want to remember a small but important task.
122|I forgot to check my bag before I left the house.|집에서 나오기 전에 가방을 확인하는 걸 깜빡했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for talking about a simple mistake before leaving.
123|I'm trying to keep my bag organized so I can find things quickly.|필요한 걸 빨리 찾을 수 있도록 가방을 정리해 두려고 해.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when describing an everyday organizing habit.
124|There are a few things in my bag that I should probably throw away.|가방 안에 이제는 버려야 할 것들이 몇 개 들어 있어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when talking about clutter and small cleanups.
125|I checked my bag twice, but I still feel like I'm forgetting something.|가방을 두 번이나 확인했는데도 아직 뭔가 빠뜨린 것 같은 기분이 들어.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when you want to express lingering uncertainty.
126|My bag gets so cluttered during the week that it slows me down every morning.|주중에 가방이 너무 어수선해져서 아침마다 준비 속도가 느려져.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when describing how small habits affect your routine.
127|I need to tidy my desk before I start working.|일을 시작하기 전에 책상을 정리해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when you need a clean space before starting.
128|I left my desk messy again last night.|어젯밤에 또 책상을 어질러 놓고 말았어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for admitting a simple habit or mistake.
129|I work better when my desk is clear and everything is in the right place.|책상이 깔끔하고 물건이 제자리에 있을 때 일이 더 잘돼.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when talking about what helps you focus.
130|My desk gets messy whenever I try to do too many things at once.|한꺼번에 너무 많은 일을 하려고 하면 책상이 금방 어질러져.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful for describing how your environment reflects your workload.
131|I finally cleared off my desk after putting it off all week.|일주일 내내 미루다가 드디어 책상을 말끔히 정리했어.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when describing delayed but satisfying progress.

132|Now that my desk is tidy, I can focus without getting distracted every few minutes.|이제 책상이 정돈돼서 몇 분마다 흐름이 끊기지 않고 집중할 수 있어.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when connecting your environment to concentration.
133|I need to charge my phone before I go to bed.|자기 전에 휴대폰을 충전해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about a basic nightly routine.
134|I forgot to charge my phone, so the battery is almost dead.|휴대폰 충전을 깜빡해서 배터리가 거의 다 닳았어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for explaining a simple everyday problem.
135|I usually charge my phone overnight so I don't have to think about it in the morning.|아침에 신경 쓰지 않으려고 보통 밤새 휴대폰을 충전해 둬.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when describing a practical habit.
136|My battery has been running out faster than usual lately.|요즘 배터리가 평소보다 더 빨리 닳고 있어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when noticing a small but annoying change.
137|I charged my phone overnight, but it still dropped below fifty percent before noon.|밤새 충전했는데도 점심도 되기 전에 배터리가 절반 아래로 떨어졌어.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when describing an unexpected device issue.
138|My phone battery drains so unpredictably that I always carry a charger just in case.|휴대폰 배터리가 너무 예측 안 되게 닳아서 혹시 몰라 항상 충전기를 들고 다녀.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when explaining a precautionary habit.
139|I need to clean my room this evening.|오늘 저녁에는 방을 청소해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about a basic home chore.
140|I haven't cleaned my room in a few days.|며칠째 방 청소를 못 했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for talking about chores you delayed.
141|I want my room to feel less cluttered before the weekend starts.|주말이 시작되기 전에 방이 덜 어수선했으면 좋겠어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when talking about preparing your space ahead of time.
142|Cleaning my room always takes longer than I think it will.|방 청소는 늘 생각보다 오래 걸려.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when talking about chores that expand unexpectedly.
143|I cleaned my room earlier, but it already looks messy again somehow.|아까 방을 치웠는데도 이상하게 벌써 다시 어질러진 것처럼 보여.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when something feels frustratingly temporary.
144|A clean room makes a bigger difference to my mood than I usually admit.|방이 깨끗하면 기분에 미치는 영향이 생각보다 훨씬 커.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when reflecting on how your space affects you.
145|I need to check my schedule for tomorrow.|내일 일정을 확인해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when planning the next day.
146|I forgot that I already had plans tomorrow morning.|내일 아침에 이미 약속이 있다는 걸 깜빡했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for talking about a scheduling mistake.
147|My schedule is starting to fill up, so I need to be more careful with my time.|일정이 점점 차기 시작해서 시간을 더 신중하게 써야 해.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when planning becomes more important.
148|I try to review my schedule at night so the next day feels less rushed.|다음 날이 덜 바쁘게 느껴지도록 밤마다 일정을 확인하려고 해.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when describing a time-management habit.
149|I've gone over my schedule several times, and it still feels too packed.|일정을 몇 번이나 다시 봤는데도 여전히 너무 빡빡하게 느껴져.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when you want to express pressure from a full calendar.

150|This week's schedule is so tight that even a small delay could throw everything off.|이번 주 일정이 너무 촘촘해서 작은 지연만 생겨도 전체가 꼬일 수 있어.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when describing how fragile a packed plan can be.
151|I need to pack my charger before I leave.|나가기 전에 충전기를 챙겨야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when remembering a basic travel or work item.
152|I almost left without my charger again.|또 충전기 없이 나갈 뻔했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for talking about a repeated small mistake.
153|I keep an extra charger in my bag because I forget things too easily.|자꾸 깜빡해서 가방에 여분 충전기를 하나 넣어 두고 다녀.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when explaining a simple preventive habit.
154|Packing the night before helps me avoid rushing around in the morning.|전날 밤에 챙겨 두면 아침에 허둥대지 않게 돼.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when talking about preparing in advance.
155|I thought I packed everything, but the charger was the one thing I overlooked.|다 챙겼다고 생각했는데 충전기만 빠뜨렸더라.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when describing a frustrating oversight.

156|Forgetting one small item can be surprisingly inconvenient when the whole day depends on it.|하루 일정이 그 물건에 달려 있을 때는 작은 물건 하나 빠뜨리는 것도 의외로 크게 불편해.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when reflecting on why small items matter.
157|I need to find my keys before we leave.|출발하기 전에 열쇠를 찾아야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when looking for something essential.
158|I left my keys somewhere in the living room.|열쇠를 거실 어딘가에 두고 왔어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for talking about misplaced items.
159|I always waste time looking for my keys when I'm already running late.|이미 늦고 있을 때마다 열쇠 찾느라 시간을 허비해.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when describing a repeated habit.
160|I should probably choose one place and always leave my keys there.|열쇠 둘 자리를 하나 정해서 항상 거기에 두는 게 좋을 것 같아.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when talking about creating a better system.

161|I found my keys in the last place I expected, which somehow made me more annoyed.|전혀 예상 못 한 곳에서 열쇠를 찾았는데 오히려 더 짜증이 났어.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when something simple becomes unexpectedly frustrating.

162|Losing my keys isn't a big disaster, but it throws off my whole rhythm for the day.|열쇠를 잃어버리는 게 큰일은 아니어도 하루 리듬이 통째로 흐트러져.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when small problems affect the rest of your day.
163|I need to update my calendar tonight.|오늘 밤에 캘린더를 업데이트해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when planning upcoming tasks or events.
164|I forgot to add that appointment to my calendar.|그 약속을 캘린더에 적는 걸 깜빡했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for talking about a simple planning mistake.
165|I rely on my calendar more when things start piling up.|일정이 쌓이기 시작하면 캘린더에 더 많이 의지하게 돼.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when describing how your planning habits change under pressure.
166|Adding even small tasks to my calendar helps me stay realistic about my time.|작은 일이라도 캘린더에 적어 두면 시간을 더 현실적으로 보게 돼.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when talking about practical time management.

167|My calendar looks manageable at first glance, but the gaps are shorter than they seem.|캘린더만 얼핏 보면 괜찮아 보이는데 실제 빈 시간은 생각보다 짧아.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when talking about hidden pressure in a schedule.

168|If I don't update my calendar right away, I end up relying on memory and forgetting important details.|캘린더를 바로바로 업데이트하지 않으면 결국 기억에만 의존하게 되고 중요한 걸 놓치게 돼.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when explaining why systems matter.
169|I need to organize my clothes before the trip.|여행 전에 옷을 정리해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when getting ready for travel or a busy week.
170|I still haven't decided which clothes to pack.|어떤 옷을 챙길지 아직도 못 정했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for talking about simple preparation.
171|I want to choose clothes that are comfortable but still look put together.|편하면서도 너무 대충 입은 것처럼 보이지 않는 옷을 고르고 싶어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when balancing comfort and appearance.

172|I tend to overpack because I keep imagining situations that probably won't happen.|아마 일어나지도 않을 상황까지 상상하면서 자꾸 짐을 너무 많이 싸게 돼.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when reflecting on a common packing habit.

173|By the time I finish organizing my clothes, I'm usually too tired to make any good decisions.|옷 정리를 다 하고 나면 늘 지쳐서 더는 제대로 판단이 안 돼.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when small tasks become mentally draining.

174|Packing would be easier if I trusted myself to make simpler choices in the first place.|애초에 더 단순하게 고를 수 있다고 스스로를 믿는다면 짐 싸는 게 훨씬 쉬울 거야.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when talking about overthinking everyday decisions.
175|I need to wipe the table after dinner.|저녁 먹고 나서 식탁을 닦아야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about a simple household task.
176|I forgot to wipe the table this morning.|오늘 아침에 식탁 닦는 걸 깜빡했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Useful for admitting a small routine mistake.
177|I try to clean the table right away so stains don't sit too long.|얼룩이 오래 남지 않게 식탁은 바로 닦으려고 해.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when describing a practical home habit.

178|If I leave the table messy, it somehow makes the whole kitchen feel unfinished.|식탁을 지저분하게 두면 부엌 전체가 정리 안 된 느낌이 들어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Useful when talking about how one detail changes the space.
179|Wiping the table is a tiny task, but it makes the room feel immediately calmer.|식탁을 닦는 건 작은 일이지만 공간이 바로 훨씬 정돈돼 보여.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when reflecting on the effect of small routines.

180|I've realized that finishing little chores right away saves more mental energy than I expected.|작은 집안일을 바로 끝내는 게 생각보다 훨씬 더 정신적 에너지를 아껴 준다는 걸 알게 됐어.|Daily life|Advanced|Useful when talking about the value of simple habits.
181|Could you explain the message again?|그 메시지를 다시 설명해 줄 수 있어?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when you need a simple clarification.
182|I just want to make sure I understood the message correctly.|내가 그 메시지를 제대로 이해했는지 확인하고 싶어.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when checking your understanding.
183|Could you help me figure out how to reply to that message?|그 메시지에 어떻게 답할지 같이 생각해 줄 수 있어?|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when asking for help with a response.
184|The message sounded a little different from what I expected.|그 메시지는 내가 예상한 것과는 조금 다르게 들렸어.|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when reacting carefully to tone or wording.

185|I don't want to overreact, but the message feels more serious than it looks at first glance.|괜히 과하게 받아들이고 싶진 않지만 그 메시지는 얼핏 보기보다 더 심각하게 느껴져.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when discussing subtext and tone.

186|Before I answer that message, I want to think through what they might actually mean.|그 메시지에 답하기 전에 상대가 실제로 무슨 뜻이었는지 먼저 생각해 보고 싶어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when you want to interpret meaning before responding.
187|Could you explain your reply one more time?|네 답장을 한 번만 더 설명해 줄래?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when the first explanation wasn't enough.
188|I wasn't sure what you meant in your reply.|네 답장 뜻을 완전히 이해하지 못했어.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when something feels unclear but not serious.
189|I want to respond politely, but I'm not sure what tone fits your reply best.|정중하게 답하고 싶은데 네 답장에는 어떤 톤이 가장 어울릴지 잘 모르겠어.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when thinking about tone in writing.

190|Your reply was short, so I wasn't sure whether you were busy or just being direct.|답장이 짧아서 네가 바쁜 건지 그냥 단도직입적인 건지 잘 모르겠었어.|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when you are unsure how to read someone's style.
191|I read your reply a few times because I didn't want to misunderstand the intention behind it.|의도를 오해하고 싶지 않아서 네 답장을 몇 번이나 다시 읽었어.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when describing careful interpretation.

192|Sometimes a brief reply leaves so much unsaid that it's harder to interpret than a long one.|짧은 답장은 말하지 않은 부분이 많아서 오히려 긴 답장보다 해석하기 어려울 때가 있어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when talking about ambiguity in communication.
193|Can we look at the schedule together?|일정을 같이 볼 수 있을까?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when starting a simple planning discussion.
194|I think I may have misunderstood the schedule.|내가 일정을 잘못 이해했을 수도 있을 것 같아.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when you think you mixed something up.
195|Could we move a few things around in the schedule?|일정에서 몇 가지만 조금 조정할 수 있을까?|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when suggesting a change without sounding forceful.
196|The schedule looks fine overall, but one part still feels too tight.|전체적으로는 괜찮아 보이는데 한 부분은 아직 너무 촉박해 보여.|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when giving balanced feedback on a plan.
197|Before we finalize the schedule, I'd like to make sure we left room for delays.|일정을 확정하기 전에 혹시 지연될 상황까지 감안했는지 확인하고 싶어.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when thinking ahead about realistic planning.

198|A schedule can look efficient on paper and still fall apart once real interruptions start happening.|일정은 서류상으로는 효율적으로 보여도 실제 변수들이 생기면 금방 무너질 수 있어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when talking about realistic expectations.
199|Could you show me the example again?|그 예시를 다시 보여줄 수 있어?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when you need to see something one more time.
200|I understand the explanation better when I look at the example.|예시를 보면 설명이 훨씬 잘 이해돼.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when talking about your learning style.
201|That example helped, but I still have one part I'm unsure about.|그 예시는 도움이 됐는데 아직 한 부분은 확신이 안 서.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when you partly understand but still need help.
202|Could you give me another example that's a little closer to my situation?|내 상황에 조금 더 가까운 예시를 하나 더 들어줄 수 있을까?|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when asking for something more relevant.

203|Examples are useful for me because they show how an idea works outside of a textbook definition.|예시는 교과서식 정의를 넘어서 개념이 실제로 어떻게 작동하는지 보여 줘서 나한테 특히 유용해.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when explaining why examples matter to you.

204|Once I saw the example in context, the whole explanation felt much less abstract.|예시가 문맥 안에서 보이니까 전체 설명이 훨씬 덜 추상적으로 느껴졌어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when talking about context improving understanding.
205|I have a quick question.|짧게 질문 하나 있어.|Conversation|Beginner|Use when opening a simple question naturally.
206|I don't think I fully understood the question.|그 질문을 완전히 이해한 건 아닌 것 같아.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when you need clarification before answering.

207|The question sounds simple, but I'm not sure what kind of answer it's really asking for.|질문 자체는 단순해 보이는데 실제로 어떤 답을 원하는 건지는 잘 모르겠어.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when a question feels broader than it looks.
208|Could you rephrase the question in a slightly different way?|그 질문을 다른 방식으로 한 번만 다시 말해 줄 수 있을까?|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when wording is the problem.

209|I want to answer carefully because the question seems more layered than it first appears.|그 질문은 처음 보이는 것보다 더 여러 층위가 있는 것 같아서 신중하게 답하고 싶어.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when you sense hidden complexity.

210|Some questions are difficult not because they're hard, but because they force you to be honest.|어떤 질문은 어려워서가 아니라 솔직해질 수밖에 없어서 답하기 힘들어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when reflecting on personal or meaningful questions.
211|Could you explain that detail again?|그 세부 사항을 다시 설명해 줄 수 있어?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when you missed a small but important point.
212|I think I missed one detail.|내가 세부 사항 하나를 놓친 것 같아.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when asking to go back briefly.
213|Most of it makes sense, but one detail still doesn't line up for me.|대부분은 이해되는데 세부 사항 하나가 아직 잘 맞아떨어지지 않아.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when one part still feels off.
214|Could we slow down and go over the details step by step?|조금 천천히 세부 사항을 하나씩 다시 볼 수 있을까?|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when you need a more careful walkthrough.

215|Small details tend to matter more than people expect once the plan actually starts moving.|계획이 실제로 움직이기 시작하면 작은 세부 사항이 생각보다 훨씬 중요해지는 경우가 많아.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when talking about why precision matters.

216|If we ignore the details now, we'll probably spend more time fixing misunderstandings later.|지금 세부 사항을 넘기면 나중에 오해를 고치느라 더 많은 시간을 쓰게 될 거야.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when arguing for careful communication.
217|I see your point.|무슨 말인지 알겠어.|Conversation|Beginner|Use when you understand someone's main idea.
218|I think I missed your point at first.|처음에는 네 요지를 놓쳤던 것 같아.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when recognizing that you misunderstood something.
219|Your main point makes sense, even if I still have questions about the details.|세부 사항에 대한 질문은 남아 있어도 네 핵심 요지는 이해돼.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when you agree broadly but not completely.
220|I agree with your point, but I see one part a little differently.|네 요지에는 동의하지만 한 부분은 조금 다르게 봐.|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when giving respectful disagreement.

221|What made your point convincing was not just the logic, but the timing of how you said it.|네 말이 설득력 있었던 건 논리뿐 아니라 그 말을 꺼낸 타이밍도 컸어.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when discussing why something resonated.
222|Sometimes the strongest point in a conversation is the one people almost overlook.|대화에서 가장 중요한 요점은 사람들이 그냥 지나칠 뻔한 부분일 때도 있어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when reflecting on subtle communication.
223|Can I call you later?|내가 나중에 전화해도 될까?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when asking to continue later by phone.
224|I missed your call earlier.|아까 네 전화를 놓쳤어.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful for explaining why you didn't answer.
225|Could we talk by phone when you have a few minutes?|몇 분 정도 시간 될 때 전화로 얘기할 수 있을까?|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when asking politely for a phone conversation.
226|I think this would be easier to explain in a call than in a text message.|이건 문자보다 전화로 설명하는 게 더 쉬울 것 같아.|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when a topic is too complex for texting.
227|Some conversations move forward much faster once you can hear the other person's tone.|상대 목소리 톤을 들을 수 있으면 어떤 대화는 훨씬 더 빨리 진전돼.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when explaining why a call may work better.
228|I hesitated to call because I didn't want to interrupt you at the wrong time.|괜히 타이밍 안 좋을 때 방해하고 싶지 않아서 전화하기를 망설였어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when expressing consideration politely.
229|Could you send the voice note again?|음성 메시지를 한 번 더 보내줄 수 있어?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when a file or clip didn't come through clearly.
230|I couldn't hear the end of the voice note very well.|음성 메시지 마지막 부분이 잘 안 들렸어.|Conversation|Beginner|Useful when audio quality is the issue.
231|The voice note helped because I could hear the tone behind the words.|말 뒤에 담긴 톤이 들려서 음성 메시지가 더 도움이 됐어.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when tone matters as much as the content.

232|Could you make the next voice note a little shorter so I can replay it more easily?|다음 음성 메시지는 내가 다시 듣기 쉽게 조금만 짧게 보내줄 수 있을까?|Conversation|Intermediate|Useful when asking for a more practical format.

233|A voice note can feel more personal, but it also leaves more room for interpretation.|음성 메시지는 더 개인적으로 느껴지지만 그만큼 해석의 여지도 더 생겨.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when talking about the tradeoffs of voice communication.

234|I replayed the voice note a few times because I wanted to catch the hesitation in your voice.|네 목소리의 망설임까지 느끼고 싶어서 음성 메시지를 몇 번이나 다시 들었어.|Conversation|Advanced|Useful when tone carries important meaning.
235|Could you send me the text one more time?|그 문자를 한 번만 더 보내줄래?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when the original message is missing or buried.
236|I saw your text, but I wasn't sure how to respond right away.|문자는 봤는데 바로 어떻게 답해야 할지 잘 모르겠더라.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when you need time before replying.
237|Your text was clear, but it raised a bigger question for me.|문자 자체는 분명했는데 오히려 더 큰 질문이 생겼어.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when one answer opens up a deeper issue.

238|Some texts are short enough to read quickly but complicated enough to think about all day.|어떤 문자는 금방 읽히지만 하루 종일 곱씹게 만들 정도로 복잡해.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when a message keeps lingering in your mind.
239|It's strange how a simple text can change the mood of the entire day.|단순한 문자 하나가 하루 전체 분위기를 바꿔 버릴 수 있다는 게 참 묘해.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when reflecting on the emotional effect of communication.
521|I need to wake up a little earlier tomorrow.|내일은 조금 더 일찍 일어나야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about a small plan for the next morning.
522|I made breakfast before everyone else got up.|다른 사람들이 일어나기 전에 아침을 준비했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when describing a simple morning routine.
523|I always feel better when I start the day without rushing.|하루를 서두르지 않고 시작하면 늘 기분이 더 좋아.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about a preferred routine.
524|If I oversleep, the rest of the morning feels out of sync.|늦잠을 자면 아침 내내 리듬이 어긋난 느낌이 들어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when describing how one delay affects the morning.
525|I try to keep my mornings simple so I don't waste energy on small decisions.|작은 결정에 에너지를 쓰지 않으려고 아침 루틴을 최대한 단순하게 유지해.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when explaining why you simplify your routine.
526|Even a calm morning can fall apart if one small thing goes wrong.|아무리 차분한 아침도 작은 일 하나만 틀어지면 금방 엉킬 수 있어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when talking about fragile routines.

527|A slower morning gives me enough space to think before the day starts demanding things from me.|조금 느긋한 아침이 있어야 하루가 나한테 이것저것 요구하기 전에 생각할 여유가 생겨.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when reflecting on why calm mornings matter.

528|I've noticed that the quality of my morning often decides how patient I am for the rest of the day.|아침이 어떻게 흘러가느냐에 따라 하루 종일 내가 얼마나 여유로운지가 결정되는 것 같아.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when connecting one habit to your overall mood.

529|When my routine is interrupted early, I spend the whole day trying to recover that sense of control.|아침 루틴이 초반부터 깨지면 하루 종일 다시 통제감을 되찾으려 애쓰게 돼.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when describing the emotional effect of disruptions.

530|It took me a while to realize that a good morning is less about productivity and more about steadiness.|좋은 아침은 생산성보다도 리듬과 안정감에 더 가깝다는 걸 깨닫는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸어.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when talking about a shift in perspective.
531|I need to buy milk and eggs on the way home.|집에 가는 길에 우유랑 달걀을 사야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when mentioning a basic shopping plan.
532|I forgot to bring the grocery list with me.|장보기 목록을 가져오는 걸 깜빡했어.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when you forget something practical.
533|I usually buy the same things every week.|나는 보통 매주 비슷한 것들을 사.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about regular habits.
534|Shopping takes longer when I go in without a clear plan.|장보기를 계획 없이 가면 시간이 더 오래 걸려.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when explaining why preparation matters.
535|I try not to shop when I'm hungry because I always buy too much.|배고플 때 장을 보러 가면 늘 너무 많이 사게 돼서 그러지 않으려고 해.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when talking about a common shopping habit.
536|The prices looked higher than I expected, so I changed what I planned to buy.|가격이 생각보다 비싸 보여서 사려던 걸 조금 바꿨어.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when adjusting a plan on the spot.

537|Grocery shopping feels simple until I start comparing value, convenience, and how long things will last.|장보기는 단순해 보이지만 가성비, 편의성, 보관 기간을 따지기 시작하면 갑자기 복잡해져.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when talking about hidden decisions in daily tasks.

538|I spend more time than I should choosing small things because I always imagine which option I'll regret later.|나중에 후회할 선택을 상상하게 돼서 작은 물건 하나 고르는 데도 생각보다 시간을 많이 써.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when describing overthinking in ordinary choices.

539|The list keeps me focused, but I still end up buying one or two things I didn't plan on.|목록이 있으면 집중은 되지만 결국 계획에 없던 것들도 한두 개씩 사게 돼.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when talking about realistic self-control.

540|I used to think grocery shopping was just an errand, but it's really a weekly decision about time, money, and energy.|예전에는 장보기가 그냥 심부름이라고 생각했는데 사실은 시간, 돈, 에너지를 어떻게 쓸지에 대한 주간 결정이더라.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when reflecting on everyday responsibilities.
541|I need to do the laundry tonight.|오늘 밤에는 빨래를 해야 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about a simple home task.
542|I left the clean clothes in the basket again.|깨끗한 빨래를 또 바구니에 그대로 뒀어.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when admitting a familiar home habit.
543|I try to finish small chores before they pile up.|작은 집안일이 쌓이기 전에 끝내려고 해.|Daily life|Beginner|Use when talking about practical routines.
544|Laundry feels easy until I wait long enough for it to turn into three different tasks.|빨래는 미루지 않을 땐 쉬운데 오래 미루면 일이 세 가지로 늘어나 버려.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when one chore becomes multiple chores.

545|I like getting chores done early because the whole evening feels lighter afterward.|집안일을 일찍 끝내면 그 뒤 저녁 시간이 훨씬 가볍게 느껴져.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when describing the reward of getting things done.
546|Some chores are repetitive, but finishing them still gives me a sense of stability.|집안일은 반복적이지만 끝내고 나면 이상하게 안정감이 생겨.|Daily life|Intermediate|Use when talking about why routine tasks still matter.

547|I keep underestimating how much mental space unfinished chores take up in the background.|끝내지 못한 집안일이 머릿속 뒤편에서 얼마나 많은 신경을 쓰게 만드는지 자꾸 과소평가하게 돼.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when talking about the hidden weight of unfinished tasks.

548|The chore itself isn't difficult; it's the transition from thinking about it to actually starting that's hard.|집안일 자체가 어려운 건 아닌데 생각만 하다가 실제로 시작하는 전환이 제일 어려워.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when describing procrastination honestly.

549|Whenever I reset the space around me, I realize how much clutter had been affecting my attention.|주변 공간을 한 번 정리하고 나면 그동안 잡동사니가 집중력에 얼마나 영향을 주고 있었는지 실감하게 돼.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when connecting physical space to focus.

550|I used to treat chores like interruptions, but now I see them as part of keeping my week from falling apart.|예전엔 집안일을 흐름을 끊는 방해라고 생각했는데 이제는 한 주를 무너지지 않게 지탱하는 일부로 보게 돼.|Daily life|Advanced|Use when talking about a mature shift in mindset.

552|I can stop by for a little while after work.|퇴근하고 잠깐 들를 수 있어.|Social|Beginner|Use when making a casual plan.
553|Let's keep it simple and meet somewhere nearby.|복잡하게 하지 말고 근처에서 만나자.|Social|Beginner|Use when suggesting an easy plan.
554|I don't mind where we go as long as we can actually talk.|어디를 가든 괜찮은데 제대로 이야기할 수만 있으면 좋겠어.|Social|Intermediate|Use when the quality of the time matters more than the place.
555|It's easier for me to commit to plans when I know the day won't be too hectic.|그날 일정이 너무 빡빡하지 않다는 걸 알면 약속을 잡기가 더 편해.|Social|Intermediate|Use when explaining how you decide about plans.

556|I like plans that leave a little room to be spontaneous instead of being fully mapped out.|너무 빡빡하게 정해진 약속보다 조금은 즉흥적으로 움직일 수 있는 계획이 좋아.|Social|Intermediate|Use when describing your preferred social style.

557|The best hangouts are usually the ones that don't demand too much but still leave you feeling recharged.|최고의 만남은 보통 큰 에너지를 요구하지 않으면서도 끝나고 나면 오히려 충전된 느낌이 드는 자리야.|Social|Advanced|Use when reflecting on what makes good time together.

558|I enjoy seeing people more when it feels like we're choosing the moment, not just filling a slot in the calendar.|단순히 빈 시간을 채우는 게 아니라 진짜 그 순간을 고른 느낌일 때 사람 만나는 게 더 좋아.|Social|Advanced|Use when talking about intentional plans.

559|Sometimes a low-key dinner tells me more about a friendship than any big event ever could.|조용한 저녁 한 끼가 때로는 어떤 큰 행사보다도 관계에 대해 더 많은 걸 말해 주기도 해.|Social|Advanced|Use when reflecting on meaningful time with people.

560|I used to say yes to every invitation, but now I care more about whether I'll be fully present when I show up.|예전에는 모든 약속에 다 응했는데 이제는 가서 내가 정말 온전히 함께할 수 있는지가 더 중요해.|Social|Advanced|Use when talking about boundaries and presence.
561|How have you been lately?|요즘 어떻게 지냈어?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when opening a casual conversation.
562|It's been a while since we last talked.|우리 마지막으로 이야기한 지 꽤 됐네.|Conversation|Beginner|Use when reconnecting with someone.
563|I was just thinking about you the other day.|며칠 전에 문득 네 생각이 났어.|Conversation|Beginner|Use when starting a warm check-in.
564|I wasn't sure whether to message you, but I'm glad I did.|연락해도 될지 잠깐 고민했는데 하길 잘한 것 같아.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when reaching out after some hesitation.
565|Sometimes I don't have big news, but I still want to stay connected.|특별한 소식은 없어도 계속 연결돼 있고 싶을 때가 있어.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when explaining why you reached out.
566|It's nice talking to someone who already understands the context without much explanation.|많이 설명하지 않아도 맥락을 알아주는 사람과 이야기하니 좋다.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when appreciating an easy connection.

567|Catching up reminds me how much can change quietly in the background without us noticing right away.|안부를 나누다 보면 우리가 바로 알아차리지 못한 사이에도 얼마나 많은 게 조용히 바뀌는지 느끼게 돼.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when reflecting during a reconnection.

568|Some conversations don't solve anything, but they still leave you feeling steadier afterward.|어떤 대화는 문제를 해결해 주진 않아도 끝나고 나면 훨씬 안정된 느낌을 남겨 줘.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when talking about emotional support.

569|I value conversations that can hold both ordinary updates and the things we're not quite ready to say directly.|평범한 근황과 아직 직접적으로 말할 준비가 안 된 이야기 둘 다 담을 수 있는 대화를 나는 소중하게 생각해.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when talking about depth in communication.

570|Staying in touch matters more to me now that I've realized how easily good relationships drift without intention.|좋은 관계도 의식하지 않으면 쉽게 멀어진다는 걸 알고 난 뒤로는 계속 연락을 주고받는 일이 더 중요해졌어.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when reflecting on maintaining relationships.
571|Can we talk about this tomorrow instead?|이 얘기는 내일 하는 걸로 할까?|Conversation|Beginner|Use when postponing a discussion simply.
572|I need a little more time to think about it.|이건 조금 더 생각할 시간이 필요해.|Conversation|Beginner|Use when you don't want to answer immediately.
573|Let's decide after we check everyone's schedule.|다들 일정 확인한 다음에 결정하자.|Conversation|Beginner|Use when a decision depends on others.
574|I don't want to rush the answer and end up saying something incomplete.|서둘러 답했다가 제대로 정리되지 않은 말을 하고 싶진 않아.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when asking for time thoughtfully.
575|We should probably revisit this once we have a clearer picture.|상황이 좀 더 분명해지면 이걸 다시 이야기해 보는 게 좋을 것 같아.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when you want to delay without dismissing the issue.
576|If we keep trying to decide too early, we'll just keep changing our minds later.|너무 이르게 결정하려고 하면 나중에 계속 다시 바꾸게 될 거야.|Conversation|Intermediate|Use when warning against premature decisions.

577|A delayed decision is frustrating, but sometimes it's still better than a rushed one built on partial information.|결정이 늦어지는 건 답답하지만, 불완전한 정보로 서두른 결정하는 것보다는 나을 때가 있어.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when defending the value of waiting.
578|I'm not avoiding the conversation; I just want to come back to it with a clearer head.|대화를 피하려는 게 아니라 좀 더 정리된 상태로 다시 이야기하고 싶을 뿐이야.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when setting a thoughtful boundary.
579|Timing changes the quality of a conversation more than people usually admit.|대화의 질은 사람들이 생각하는 것보다 타이밍에 더 크게 좌우돼.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when explaining why now may not be the best time.

580|Sometimes giving an issue one more night creates the distance needed to see it more honestly.|문제를 하룻밤 더 두는 것만으로도 더 솔직하게 볼 수 있는 거리가 생길 때가 있어.|Conversation|Advanced|Use when reflecting on delayed clarity.
581|Which bus goes to the station?|어떤 버스를 타야 역으로 가?|Travel|Beginner|Use when asking for a simple direction.
582|I think we got off one stop too early.|우리 한 정거장 먼저 내린 것 같아.|Travel|Beginner|Use when you think you made a small transit mistake.
583|The station is closer than I expected.|역이 생각보다 더 가깝네.|Travel|Beginner|Use when the destination is nearby.
584|The route is simple once you know where to transfer.|어디서 갈아타는지만 알면 경로는 간단해.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when explaining transit to someone.
585|Traffic was heavier than usual, so the whole commute took longer.|평소보다 차가 막혀서 이동 시간이 전체적으로 더 길어졌어.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when explaining an unexpected delay.
586|I don't mind a longer commute if I can actually sit down and think for a while.|앉아서 잠깐 생각할 수만 있다면 이동 시간이 좀 길어도 괜찮아.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when sharing a more reflective view of commuting.

587|Commuting feels much shorter when I know exactly where I'm going and what I'm doing once I get there.|어디로 가는지, 도착해서 뭘 할지 분명할 때는 이동 시간도 훨씬 짧게 느껴져.|Travel|Advanced|Use when talking about the psychology of travel.

588|The most stressful part of commuting isn't the distance but the uncertainty between each step.|출퇴근이나 이동에서 가장 스트레스인 건 거리가 아니라 다음 단계가 얼마나 불확실한지인 것 같아.|Travel|Advanced|Use when reflecting on what makes travel tiring.

589|Once a route becomes familiar, you stop noticing how many tiny decisions it used to require.|길이 익숙해지고 나면 예전에는 얼마나 많은 작은 결정을 해야 했는지조차 잊게 돼.|Travel|Advanced|Use when talking about habit and navigation.

590|I always feel calmer once I've made the first transfer and know the rest of the trip will unfold predictably.|첫 번째 환승만 무사히 끝나면 나머지 이동은 예상대로 흘러갈 거라는 생각에 훨씬 마음이 놓여.|Travel|Advanced|Use when describing the moment uncertainty fades.
591|What time is the flight tomorrow?|내일 비행기 몇 시야?|Travel|Beginner|Use when checking a basic travel detail.
592|I need to confirm the hotel reservation tonight.|오늘 밤에 호텔 예약을 다시 확인해야 해.|Travel|Beginner|Use when planning a trip.
593|I packed most of what I need already.|필요한 건 거의 다 챙겨 뒀어.|Travel|Beginner|Use when you're almost ready to go.
594|I always feel better once the booking details are in one place.|예약 정보가 한곳에 정리돼 있으면 마음이 훨씬 편해져.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when talking about travel preparation.
595|The hotel looked great in the photos, but I want to keep my expectations realistic.|호텔 사진은 정말 좋아 보였지만 기대는 조금 현실적으로 하려고 해.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when balancing excitement with caution.

596|Travel goes more smoothly when I leave room for small delays instead of planning everything too tightly.|모든 걸 너무 빡빡하게 짜기보다는 작은 지연을 감안해 두는 편이 여행이 더 부드럽게 흘러가.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when talking about travel planning style.

597|I don't get anxious about the flight itself as much as the chain of little things around it.|비행 자체보다 그 전후에 딸려 있는 자잘한 일들이 더 긴장돼.|Travel|Advanced|Use when describing what travel stress actually feels like.

599|Part of traveling well is accepting that not everything needs to go exactly as imagined.|여행을 잘한다는 건 모든 일이 상상한 그대로 흘러갈 필요는 없다는 걸 받아들이는 일인 것 같아.|Travel|Advanced|Use when talking about flexibility.

616|The update is less dramatic than it looks; we mostly spent time reducing future confusion.|이번 업데이트는 겉보기만큼 큰 변화라기보다 앞으로의 혼선을 줄이는 데 시간을 많이 썼어요.|Work|Intermediate|Use when explaining less-visible progress.

618|The main challenge isn't finishing the work itself but making sure the next person can build on it smoothly.|가장 큰 과제는 일을 끝내는 것 자체보다 다음 사람이 자연스럽게 이어받을 수 있게 만드는 거예요.|Work|Advanced|Use when focusing on handoff quality.

620|I've learned that a responsible status update includes not only what changed, but also what remains uncertain.|책임 있는 진행 공유에는 바뀐 것뿐 아니라 아직 불확실한 부분도 함께 담겨야 한다는 걸 배웠어요.|Work|Advanced|Use when describing mature communication at work.

625|I don't mind more discussion, as long as it leads to a concrete decision.|논의가 더 길어져도 괜찮은데 결국 구체적인 결정으로 이어지기만 하면 돼.|Work|Intermediate|Use when balancing patience with practicality.

626|Meetings feel more productive when everyone understands what kind of decision is actually being asked for.|회의는 지금 어떤 종류의 결정을 해야 하는지 모두가 이해할 때 더 생산적으로 느껴져.|Work|Intermediate|Use when talking about meeting clarity.

627|The conversation was thoughtful, but it kept circling the issue instead of resolving the tradeoff at the center of it.|대화는 깊이 있었지만 핵심 쟁점을 정리하기보다 주변을 맴도는 느낌이었어.|Work|Advanced|Use when evaluating a discussion honestly.

628|A meeting can be calm and respectful while still avoiding the one question that actually matters.|회의가 차분하고 존중하는 분위기라고 해서 정말 중요한 질문을 다루고 있다는 뜻은 아니야.|Work|Advanced|Use when reflecting on unspoken avoidance.

629|What makes a decision difficult isn't always complexity; sometimes it's the cost of choosing one priority over another.|결정을 어렵게 만드는 건 언제나 복잡성만이 아니라 한 우선순위를 택하면서 다른 걸 포기해야 하는 대가일 때도 있어.|Work|Advanced|Use when discussing tradeoffs.
630|The most useful meetings are the ones that leave less ambiguity, not just more shared airtime.|가장 유용한 회의는 모두가 말을 많이 한 회의가 아니라 애매함을 덜 남기는 회의야.|Work|Advanced|Use when reflecting on meeting quality.

635|The feedback was helpful because it showed me where the audience might lose the thread.|어느 부분에서 청중이 흐름을 놓칠 수 있는지 보여 줘서 피드백이 도움이 됐어.|Work|Intermediate|Use when explaining what made feedback useful.
636|The slides look polished, but I still want the spoken explanation to feel more natural.|슬라이드는 잘 다듬어졌지만 말로 설명하는 흐름은 더 자연스러웠으면 해.|Work|Intermediate|Use when distinguishing design from delivery.

637|A presentation becomes persuasive when the structure quietly guides the audience before they even notice it.|발표는 구조가 청중을 눈치채지 못할 정도로 자연스럽게 이끌 때 더 설득력을 갖게 돼.|Work|Advanced|Use when talking about high-level presentation quality.

638|Good feedback doesn't just point out what feels weak; it reveals why the message isn't landing yet.|좋은 피드백은 약한 부분만 지적하는 게 아니라 왜 메시지가 아직 제대로 닿지 않는지도 보여 줘.|Work|Advanced|Use when reflecting on valuable critique.

639|What I'm revising now isn't the information itself but the order that allows it to feel inevitable.|지금 내가 고치는 건 정보 자체가 아니라 그 정보가 자연스럽고 필연적으로 느껴지게 만드는 순서야.|Work|Advanced|Use when talking about refinement in communication.

648|What I need isn't just more repetition, but a better sense of why the rule changes in different contexts.|내게 필요한 건 단순 반복이 아니라 왜 규칙이 문맥에 따라 달라지는지에 대한 더 나은 감각이야.|Study|Advanced|Use when talking about deeper understanding.

654|Taking notes helps me stay engaged when the reading is dense.|내용이 빽빽할 때는 필기를 해야 집중이 유지돼.|Study|Intermediate|Use when describing how you handle difficult reading.
655|I remember more from a chapter when I summarize it in my own words right away.|챕터를 바로 내 말로 정리하면 기억에 훨씬 오래 남아.|Study|Intermediate|Use when talking about effective review.

656|Some passages only become clear after I stop reading for answers and start reading for structure.|어떤 지문은 답을 찾으려는 마음을 내려놓고 구조를 보기 시작해야 비로소 분명해져.|Study|Intermediate|Use when your reading strategy shifts.

657|The article wasn't difficult because of the vocabulary alone; it was difficult because every paragraph quietly shifted the argument.|그 글이 어려웠던 건 단어 때문만이 아니라 문단마다 논지의 방향이 미묘하게 달라졌기 때문이야.|Study|Advanced|Use when explaining why a text felt challenging.

658|Good notes don't just repeat the text; they capture what changed in my understanding while I was reading it.|좋은 필기는 본문을 그대로 옮기는 게 아니라 읽는 동안 내 이해가 어떻게 바뀌었는지를 남겨 줘.|Study|Advanced|Use when reflecting on what makes notes valuable.

659|Rereading becomes useful once I'm paying attention to how the writer builds pressure rather than just what they state.|다시 읽기는 글쓴이가 무엇을 말했는지만이 아니라 어떻게 논리를 쌓아 가는지를 볼 때 훨씬 유용해져.|Study|Advanced|Use when reading more analytically.

666|Speaking practice feels less intimidating when I aim for clarity before speed.|말하기 연습은 속도보다 명확함에 먼저 집중하면 덜 부담스러워.|Study|Intermediate|Use when explaining your approach to speaking.

669|The gap between understanding and producing a language is frustrating precisely because it feels so close.|언어를 이해하는 것과 실제로 말로 꺼내는 것 사이의 차이는 거의 될 것 같아서 더 답답해.|Study|Advanced|Use when reflecting on language learning frustration.

670|Progress in listening and speaking is gradual enough that I have to notice it indirectly through what feels less exhausting than before.|듣기와 말하기의 진전은 너무 서서히 일어나서 예전보다 덜 지치는 부분을 통해 간접적으로 알아차려야 할 때가 많아.|Study|Advanced|Use when reflecting on subtle progress.

675|Consistency feels boring in the moment, but it creates results that emotion alone usually can't.|꾸준함은 순간순간 지루하게 느껴질 수 있지만 감정만으로는 만들기 어려운 결과를 만들어 줘.|Opinion|Intermediate|Use when comparing discipline and emotion.

676|I've learned that progress becomes visible only after you keep going past the point where it still feels insignificant.|진전은 아직 별것 아닌 것처럼 느껴지는 지점을 지나서도 계속해야 비로소 눈에 보인다는 걸 배웠어.|Opinion|Intermediate|Use when reflecting on persistence.

677|A sustainable habit isn't the one that looks impressive for a week; it's the one that survives an ordinary Tuesday.|지속 가능한 습관은 일주일 동안 대단해 보이는 게 아니라 평범한 화요일에도 살아남는 습관이야.|Opinion|Advanced|Use when defining sustainability in a memorable way.

678|I trust progress more when it grows quietly in the background than when it arrives with a burst of enthusiasm.|열정이 폭발하듯 오는 변화보다 조용히 배경에서 쌓이는 진전을 나는 더 믿게 돼.|Opinion|Advanced|Use when comparing flashy motivation and steady effort.

679|People often talk about discipline as if it's harsh, but for me it feels more like a way of reducing friction.|사람들은 규율을 엄격한 걸로 말하곤 하지만 나에게는 오히려 마찰을 줄이는 방식에 더 가까워.|Opinion|Advanced|Use when reframing discipline.

684|Technology is helpful when it removes friction instead of creating more decisions.|기술은 결정을 더 늘리는 대신 마찰을 줄여 줄 때 유용해.|Opinion|Intermediate|Use when sharing a more thoughtful opinion about tools.

685|A clean interface matters because it affects how willing I am to return to something every day.|깔끔한 인터페이스는 매일 다시 돌아오고 싶은 마음에 영향을 주기 때문에 중요해.|Opinion|Intermediate|Use when talking about design from a user's perspective.

686|The best tools don't demand constant attention; they quietly support what you're already trying to do.|최고의 도구는 계속 관심을 요구하지 않고 이미 하려던 일을 조용히 뒷받침해 줘.|Opinion|Intermediate|Use when describing what makes a product useful.

687|Convenience becomes powerful when it protects attention rather than simply speeding up more activity.|편리함은 단순히 더 많은 일을 빠르게 하게 만드는 게 아니라 주의를 지켜 줄 때 더 강력해져.|Opinion|Advanced|Use when reflecting on the real value of convenience.

688|A product can be full of features and still feel poorly designed if it constantly interrupts the user's intention.|기능이 많아도 사용자의 의도를 계속 끊는다면 잘 설계된 제품이라고 느껴지지 않아.|Opinion|Advanced|Use when critiquing design philosophy.

689|I trust tools more when they make my behavior easier to understand instead of merely trying to capture more time from me.|내 시간을 더 오래 붙잡으려 하기보다 내 행동을 더 잘 이해하게 해 주는 도구를 나는 더 신뢰해.|Opinion|Advanced|Use when comparing support and attention capture.

690|Good design often feels invisible because it lets the user stay focused on the purpose rather than the product itself.|좋은 디자인은 사용자가 제품 자체보다 목적에 집중하게 만들어서 오히려 잘 보이지 않는 경우가 많아.|Opinion|Advanced|Use when reflecting on subtle design quality.
691|It was nice seeing everyone again.|다시 다 같이 보니까 좋았어.|Social|Beginner|Use when talking after a gathering.
692|We should do this again sometime.|우리 다음에 또 이런 시간 가지자.|Social|Beginner|Use when ending a casual gathering warmly.
693|I'm glad I decided to come.|오길 잘한 것 같아.|Social|Beginner|Use when a plan turned out well.
694|I almost canceled, but I'm happy I made the effort.|거의 취소할 뻔했는데 그래도 나오길 잘했어.|Social|Intermediate|Use when a plan felt worth the effort.
695|The best part was how easy the conversation felt once everyone settled in.|다들 편해지고 나니까 대화가 자연스럽게 이어진 게 가장 좋았어.|Social|Intermediate|Use when reflecting on why a gathering felt good.
696|I like plans that feel relaxed enough for people to be more like themselves.|사람들이 더 자기답게 있을 수 있을 만큼 편안한 약속이 좋아.|Social|Intermediate|Use when describing your ideal kind of social time.

697|What makes a gathering memorable usually isn't the event itself, but the feeling people leave with afterward.|어떤 모임이 기억에 남는 건 보통 행사 자체보다 끝나고 난 뒤 사람들이 안고 가는 분위기 때문이야.|Social|Advanced|Use when reflecting on shared experiences.

698|A good social space gives people room to talk without making them feel like they have to perform.|좋은 모임 분위기는 사람들에게 말할 공간을 주면서도 뭔가 보여 줘야 한다는 부담은 덜어 줘.|Social|Advanced|Use when talking about comfortable group settings.

699|I've started valuing gatherings more when they leave me feeling connected rather than simply entertained.|단순히 재미있었다기보다 연결된 느낌을 남기는 모임을 더 소중하게 여기게 됐어.|Social|Advanced|Use when describing what matters more over time.

700|Sometimes the quietest part of an evening tells you the most about how comfortable people really feel together.|어떤 저녁의 가장 조용한 순간이 오히려 사람들이 얼마나 편한지 가장 잘 보여 줄 때가 있어.|Social|Advanced|Use when reflecting on group dynamics.
701|I want to plan one short trip before summer ends.|여름이 끝나기 전에 짧은 여행 하나는 가고 싶어.|Travel|Beginner|Use when sharing a simple travel wish.
702|The photos made the place look quieter than I expected.|사진으로 보니 그곳이 생각보다 더 한적해 보였어.|Travel|Beginner|Use when reacting to a destination.
703|I don't need a long trip, just a change of scenery.|긴 여행까지는 아니어도 풍경이 좀 바뀌면 좋겠어.|Travel|Beginner|Use when talking about wanting a short break.
704|A short trip feels more manageable when the travel itself isn't too tiring.|이동 자체가 너무 힘들지 않을 때 짧은 여행이 훨씬 더 가볍게 느껴져.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when describing what makes a trip worth it.
705|I enjoy travel more when the schedule leaves room to wander a little.|여행 일정에 조금은 헤맬 여유가 있을 때 더 즐겁더라.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when describing your travel style.
706|The places I remember best are usually the ones where I felt less rushed than usual.|가장 오래 기억에 남는 여행지는 보통 평소보다 덜 쫓기던 곳이야.|Travel|Intermediate|Use when reflecting on memorable travel.

707|A good trip doesn't have to be packed with activities if it gives you enough distance to notice your own thoughts again.|좋은 여행은 일정이 빼곡하지 않아도 다시 자기 생각을 들여다볼 만큼의 거리를 만들어 주면 충분해.|Travel|Advanced|Use when talking about the deeper value of travel.

708|What I look for in travel now is not constant excitement but a pace that lets my attention settle.|요즘 여행에서 내가 찾는 건 계속되는 자극이 아니라 내 주의가 가라앉을 수 있는 속도야.|Travel|Advanced|Use when describing a more mature travel preference.

709|The most restorative trips are often the ones that interrupt familiar patterns without overwhelming me with novelty.|가장 회복되는 여행은 익숙한 패턴은 끊어 주면서도 새로운 자극으로 나를 압도하지는 않는 여행인 것 같아.|Travel|Advanced|Use when reflecting on restorative travel.

710|Travel feels meaningful to me when I come back with a quieter mind, not just more photos.|나에게 의미 있는 여행은 사진이 더 많이 남는 여행이 아니라 마음이 더 조용해져서 돌아오는 여행이야.|Travel|Advanced|Use when explaining what travel means to you.
711|I want to keep studying even on busy days.|바쁜 날에도 공부를 계속 이어 가고 싶어.|Study|Beginner|Use when talking about consistency.
712|Ten minutes is better than doing nothing.|10분이라도 하는 게 아예 안 하는 것보단 나아.|Study|Beginner|Use when encouraging small progress.
713|I try to review a little every day.|매일 조금씩이라도 복습하려고 해.|Study|Beginner|Use when describing a simple habit.
714|Short study sessions work better for me when my schedule is unpredictable.|일정이 들쭉날쭉할 때는 짧게 나눠서 공부하는 게 나한테 더 잘 맞아.|Study|Intermediate|Use when explaining what fits your routine.

715|What helps most is lowering the barrier to starting, not raising the pressure to do more.|가장 도움이 되는 건 더 많이 하라고 압박하는 게 아니라 시작 장벽을 낮추는 거야.|Study|Intermediate|Use when talking about sustainable study habits.

716|I don't always need a perfect plan; sometimes I just need a reliable next step.|언제나 완벽한 계획이 필요한 건 아니고 그냥 믿고 따라갈 수 있는 다음 한 걸음이 필요할 때가 있어.|Study|Intermediate|Use when talking about practical progress.

717|Consistency becomes easier once I stop measuring each day by intensity and start measuring it by return.|꾸준함은 매일의 강도로 스스로를 재지 않고 다시 돌아오는 횟수로 보기 시작하면 훨씬 쉬워져.|Study|Advanced|Use when reframing consistency.

718|The goal isn't to feel inspired every day, but to make it easy enough to continue when inspiration is missing.|목표는 매일 영감을 느끼는 게 아니라 영감이 없을 때도 계속할 수 있을 만큼 쉽게 만들어 두는 거야.|Study|Advanced|Use when reflecting on sustainable discipline.

719|A learning habit becomes real when it survives boredom, distraction, and the urge to start over dramatically.|배움의 습관은 지루함과 산만함, 그리고 갑자기 처음부터 다시 시작하고 싶은 충동까지 견뎌 낼 때 비로소 진짜가 돼.|Study|Advanced|Use when talking about long-term persistence.

720|I've started to trust the kind of progress that looks almost invisible from day to day but becomes undeniable over months.|하루 단위로 보면 거의 보이지 않지만 몇 달이 지나면 분명해지는 그런 진전을 이제는 더 신뢰하게 됐어.|Study|Advanced|Use when reflecting on long-term growth.
